heading: Penalty and forfeiture for removal of liquors under improper brands
label: 5683 Penalty and forfeiture for removal of liquors under improper brands
text: Whenever any person ships, transports, or removes any distilled spirits, wines, or beer, under any other than the proper name or brand known to the trade as designating the kind and quality of the contents of the casks or packages containing the same, or causes such act to be done, he shall be fined not more than $1,000, or imprisoned not more than 1 year, or both, and shall forfeit such distilled spirits, wines, or beer, and casks or packages.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 5683, , , consisted of provisions similar to those comprising this section, prior to the general revision of this chapter by .act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 699Pub. L. 85–859
